questions.question: The 'golden hour' for landscape photography is…
options: Shortly after sunrise and before sunset, when light is warm and soft
Midday when the sun is highest
Any time on an overcast day
questions.reviewHint: Golden hour around sunrise/sunset gives warm, soft, directional light ideal for landscapes.

questions.question: Why is midday light often less ideal for landscapes?
options: It is harsh and overhead, creating flat or high-contrast results
questions.reviewHint: Overhead midday sun is harsh and can flatten a scene or create unflattering contrast.

questions.question: For front-to-back sharpness in a landscape, photographers typically use…
options: A small aperture such as f/8-f/11
questions.reviewHint: Small apertures (f/8-f/11) give the deep depth of field landscapes usually need.

questions.question: A tripod is valuable in landscape photography because it…
options: Allows sharp images at slow shutter speeds and careful composition
questions.reviewHint: A tripod enables slow shutter speeds, precise framing and sharp results.

questions.question: A graduated neutral density (grad ND) filter is used to…
options: Balance a bright sky against a darker foreground
questions.reviewHint: A grad ND darkens the bright sky to balance it with the land in one exposure.

questions.question: A standard (non-graduated) ND filter helps landscapes by…
options: Allowing longer exposures (e.g. to blur water or clouds)
questions.reviewHint: A plain ND cuts light so you can use long shutter speeds for motion blur in water/cloud.

questions.question: Including a strong foreground element in a landscape mainly helps to…
options: Add depth and lead the eye into the scene
questions.reviewHint: A foreground anchor creates depth and draws the viewer into the frame.

questions.question: Which focal length range is commonly associated with classic wide landscapes?
questions.reviewHint: Wide-angle lenses capture expansive scenes, though telephotos are also used to isolate.

questions.question: 'Blue hour' refers to…
options: The period of soft blue light just before sunrise or after sunset
questions.reviewHint: Blue hour is the twilight period giving cool, soft, even light.

questions.question: To keep the horizon looking professional, you should…
options: Keep it level (straight) unless deliberately tilting for effect
Place it exactly in the centre every time
questions.reviewHint: A level horizon avoids a distracting tilt; placement is usually on a third.

questions.question: A polarising filter is useful in landscapes to…
questions.reviewHint: A polariser reduces glare/reflections and can make skies richer.
